plan_metadata.name: group  debug in teams meeting remote

steps.description: Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the command palette.
Type 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:'Launch Remote (Chrome)' into the debug configuration dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface, within the Microsoft 365 Agents project workspace.
Press Enter to select "Launch Remote (Chrome)" from the debug configuration dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field on the Microsoft sign-in page within the Chrome browser.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the 'Email, phone, or Skype' input field on the Microsoft sign-in page in Google Chrome.
Click the blue "Next" button on the Microsoft sign-in page after entering the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Type  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the password input field on the Microsoft sign-in page for user  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}}.
Click the blue "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page after entering the password for the account  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}}.
Click the "Yes" button on the Microsoft sign-in prompt to confirm staying signed in for account  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Click the “Add” button within the app details  in Microsoft Teams (web interface), indicated by a blue button below the app name and publisher.
Click inside the "Search for a channel or meeting" input box in the "Added successfully!" pop-up for the hello-world-in-meetingdev app on the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Type the text 'testing' into the search input field within the "Added successfully!" dialog of the hello-world-in-meetingdev app on Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the radio button next to the "testin9" meeting entry in the "Choose where to get started" dialog on the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the radio button next to "testing" under the Meetings section in the "Added successfully!" dialog of the hello-world-in-meetingdev app in Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Click the "Go" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog to confirm adding the app in the Microsoft Teams web interface.
